origin.name: Malaysia
origin.key_document: Malaysian death certificate (bilingual English and Malay)
origin.source: FCDO Malaysia travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Kuala Lumpur guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Malaysia is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is often simpler than full body repatriation. You will need the local death certificate, cremation certificate, and export documentation. Muslim deaths have additional religious considerations.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Malaysian death certificate (bilingual English and Malay)
special_rules: Malaysia issues death certificates in both English and Malay, which simplifies UK documentation requirements.
Muslim deaths have additional religious and procedural considerations that affect preparation and timing.
Police clearance is required for all violent or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from Malaysian authorities is required for all repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Malaysian death certificates are issued in English, so documentation is straightforward. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Kuala Lumpur can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Malaysia to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Malaysia. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. English death certificates, export permits, and air cargo all handled. Contact us 24/7.
